definition: MN 137's category of distress arising from longing for unsurpassed release before it has been fully entered.
notes: Use this record for the renunciation-side distress family in MN 137. Keep the renunciation tie explicit and keep the line practical: the distress belongs to longing for release, not to failure of practice. Do not flatten it into vague devotional sadness.

example_phrases.notes: Category label in the thirty-six positions section of MN 137.

authority_basis.scope: Supports the renunciation-side distress family in the six-fields analysis.

translation_policy.default_scope: Use this as the standard English label for the nekkhammasita domanassa category in MN 137 unless a recorded local reason requires minor grammatical adjustment.
translation_policy.when_not_to_apply: Do not flatten the category into generic sadness language or lose the renunciation-side distinction.
translation_policy.drift_risk: Prevents the MN 137 category from drifting into vague spiritualized sorrow language or losing the renunciation-side tie.
